summary: A superb soundboard recording of a groovy 1984 Marin show, highlighted by an incendiary Scarlet>Fire and soaring Fire on the Mountain.
review: The board‑tape captured by Charlie Miller is crystal‑clear and lovingly remastered, letting the band’s relaxed yet tight playing shine through. Reviewers rave about the blistering Scarlet>Fire, the powerful Fire on the Mountain and He's Gone, plus rare moments like Alabama Getaway into Feel Like a Stranger. The performance feels loose and fun with Brent Mydland’s synth‑rich keyboards driving many of the jams.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both musical performance (standout songs, tight groove) and technical quality (excellent soundboard), with only minor criticisms.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remastered board tape is crisp, balanced and free of noticeable drop‑outs; reviewers call it "A‑1" and note the excellent acoustics of the venue.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A relaxed, groove‑heavy first set with tight transitions; the second set builds into an ecstatic Scarlet>Fire and finishes with a solid The Other One/T‑Stones combo.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals are surprisingly strong (Tom Waits‑ish) and his lead on Fire is fiery; guitar work holds together well.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s synth/vibe work dominates the Scarlet>Fire, adding island‑rhythmed riffs and a playful feel throughout.
